definition.en: The fourth inner sense is the conceptual sense.
definition.zh: 第四种内在感官是概念感官。

definition.en: It involves experiencing a concept completely, to the extent of being a concept completely.
definition.zh: 它涉及完全体验一个概念，以至于完全成为那个概念。

definition.en: You cannot truly understand or appreciate any other thing unless you can become that thing.
definition.zh: 除非你能成为那个事物，否则你无法真正理解或欣赏任何其他事物。

explanation: 概念感知是第四种内在感官。它不是用智力去理解一个概念，而是直接认知并完全体验它，甚至达到“成为”这个概念的程度。要真正理解任何事物，你必须能够变成那个事物本身，否则你得到的只是近似和扭曲的认知。

quotes.text: The fourth inner sense is the conceptual sense. Now you think of a concept in terms of an idea, which you can only understand in intellectual terms. However, the fourth inner sense involves again direct cognition, only now of a concept in much more than you would call intellectual terms; It involves experiencing a concept completely, to the extent of being a concept completely.
quotes.translation: 第四个内在感官是概念感官。现在你们以观念来思考概念，而你们只能以知性来理解观念。然而，第四个内在感官再次涉及直接认知，只是这次对概念的认知远超你们所谓的知性范畴；它涉及完全体验一个概念，甚至达到完全成为那个概念的程度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You cannot truly understand or appreciate any other thing unless you can become that thing. This is definite. Otherwise you only receive an approximation and a distortion.
quotes.translation: 除非你能成为任何其他事物，否则你无法真正理解或欣赏它。这是确定的。否则你只能接收到近似和扭曲的认知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json
